india_trends.title: Old stock turns gold for retailers as smartphone prices rise amid memory cost inflation
india_trends.summary: As memory prices experience inflation, the landscape of India's smartphone market is shifting. Retailers momentarily benefit from increased profits, pushing older models at inflated prices, while distributors are left juggling higher investments in stock and stricter credit terms.

india_trends.title: IHCL shifts focus to overseas expansion as domestic business runs on autopilot
india_trends.summary: The Indian Hotels Company, affectionately known as IHCL, is setting its sights on global horizons. With its domestic operations thriving, the company is primed for international growth through management contracts and operating leases.

india_trends.title: Bullion industry proposes plan to turn India's idle gold into working capital
india_trends.summary: India's gold imports are rising, prompting industry bodies to suggest ways to curb them. Proposals include recycling household gold and limiting the use of imported gold to jewellery exports. Jewellers are being asked to participate in gold monetisation programs. These initiatives aim to reduce the

india_trends.title: Quiet moves by corporate giant spark buzz in India’s alcobev and drinks industry
india_trends.summary: In a surprising turn of events, a prominent Indian firm is said to be eyeing the bustling beverage market. With a series of discussions involving global alcohol companies and local beverage artisans, along with the onboarding of top-tier talent, chatter is rife about their potential plans.

india_trends.title: IBDF joins legal battle over streaming of TV channels on Prasar Bharati’s WAVES platform
india_trends.summary: The Indian Broadcasting and Digital Foundation has joined a legal battle concerning Prasar Bharati's WAVES platform. The dispute centers on whether OTT services can stream live TV channels. The All India Digital Cable Federation argues against this, citing distribution rules. Prasar Bharati contends

india_trends.title: Henkel to end Pril, Fa licensing deal with Jyothy Labs after 15 years
india_trends.summary: Jyothy Labs will see its partnership with global firm Henkel conclude after May 31. Licensing agreements for Pril dishwashing and Fa personal care brands will not be renewed. While this may impact near-term revenue and margins, Jyothy Labs' diversified portfolio and strong distribution network are e

india_trends.title: Net interest margin to fall, Bank of Baroda can look to upsize treasury, wealth business
india_trends.summary: Bank of Baroda anticipates tighter margins this fiscal. Sticky deposit rates and restricted lending rate increases will impact profitability. The bank plans to enhance fee income to offset this. A new primary dealership business will support debt capital market expansion. Efforts are underway to str

india_trends.title: Natural gas mineral firm WhiteHawk reveals sevenfold revenue surge in US IPO filing
india_trends.summary: WhiteHawk, a leading natural gas royalty firm, is taking the stock market plunge with its initial public offering. india_trends.title: Syrma SGS Q4 Results: Net profit surges 67% to ₹119 crore, driven by strong revenue growth
india_trends.summary: Syrma SGS Technology Ltd experienced an impressive financial year, reporting a remarkable 87% increase in net profit, reaching Rs 345.8 crore. The company's revenue saw substantial growth as well, with export revenue exceeding Rs 1,200 crore.

india_trends.title: SBI chairman says rapid rise of AI-driven lending brings new vulnerabilities for banks
india_trends.summary: SBI Chairman CS Setty highlighted the dual nature of digital finance growth, noting both opportunities and vulnerabilities like cyber risks and algorithmic biases. He stressed that trust must be the foundation of India's expanding financial system, emphasizing that innovation should not compromise s

india_trends.title: REC arm makes move for carbon market play
india_trends.summary: REC's consulting arm is set to enter the carbon markets. It will operate as an accredited carbon verification agency. Expressions of interest are invited for agencies to join the Indian carbon market. This market is expected to launch in 2026. Empanelled agencies will conduct validation and verifica

india_trends.title: India’s new FTAs offer investment opportunities, but utilisation remains weak: Commerce Secretary
india_trends.summary: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal urged industries to leverage India's newly finalised free trade agreements, noting a historical underutilization of such pacts. While FTAs have boosted trade volumes, exports have lagged behind imports, potentially due to strong domestic demand in India's rapidly gr

india_trends.title: India plans ‘Made in India’ brand scheme, targets gaps in domestic manufacturing
india_trends.summary: India is identifying 100 products for enhanced domestic manufacturing, including auto parts, to boost production for global markets. A 'Made in India Brand Scheme' is planned, following a successful pilot in the steel sector, to assure quality and value addition. Companies are urged to invest long-t

india_trends.title: Pimco Scorns Daily Marks on Private Assets That Apollo Heralded
india_trends.summary: More frequently marking assets does little to improve transparency or accuracy in the $1.8 trillion private credit market, according to Pacific Investment Management Co. strategist Lotfi Karoui.

China-like grid planning needed to meet renewable energy goal: MNRE secretary
india_trends.summary: India aims for 1,800 gigawatts of renewable energy by 2050. Achieving this requires a super grid, similar to China's. The government plans to spend $574 billion by 2030 on high-voltage lines. Enhanced grid infrastructure will address power evacuation challenges. Policymakers are working with states

india_trends.title: India-US trade talks set for next round, but Section 301 probe remains hurdle
india_trends.summary: India anticipates a US trade delegation for upcoming talks, which will address all outstanding issues, including the Section 301 probe. A proposed trade deal with the US will not progress until this investigation concludes. The US initiated the Section 301 investigation in March, alleging India's ex

india_trends.title: What should be the trading strategy for jewellery stocks after Modi’s remarks and sharp correction?
india_trends.summary: Jewellery stocks like Kalyan Jewellers, Titan Company, and Senco Gold fell up to 10% after PM Modi urged avoiding non-essential gold purchases to protect foreign reserves. Analysts suggest long-term investors stay invested despite short-term volatility.

india_trends.title: Portugal eyes bigger role in India’s infrastructure, manufacturing push as trade ties deepen
india_trends.summary: Portugal's Minister of State for Economy, João Rui Ferreira, expressed readiness to contribute to India's infrastructure goals, highlighting strong India-Portugal trade ties. He emphasized opportunities in renewable energy, IT, textiles, and advanced manufacturing, advocating for joint ventures and

india_trends.title: What does PM Modi's work-from-home call mean for the Indian stock market?
india_trends.summary: PM Modi's call for work-from-home amid rising fuel costs reflects concerns about India's economy. Analysts suggest it indicates a prolonged oil shock, with potential impacts on the rupee and fiscal deficit, and urge caution in investments.
